resources.string: No file manager app found on this device
Maximum message size accepted by this relay
Remove %1$s from your contacts? You can re-add them later from the Announce Stream.
Filter by receiving interface (none selected = show all):
Remove %1$s from the list? They will reappear when they announce again.
This will remove all discovered nodes from the list, except those saved in My Contacts. Nodes will reappear when they announce again.
Are you sure you want to delete your conversation with %1$s? This will permanently delete all messages.
They will no longer be able to send you messages. Their conversation will be hidden from the chat list.
Also blackhole (do not relay their announces)
Transport is currently disabled. This identity will be blackholed whenever transport is later enabled.
Messages from peers will appear here
Save to a folder on your device
Search to find any of %1$d icons
Optional: Sets the virtual network name for this segment. This allows multiple separate networks to exist on the same physical channel or medium.
Optional: Sets an authentication passphrase on the interface. This can be used in conjunction with Network Name, or alone.
Route through a SOCKS5 proxy (e.g., Orbot for Tor). Required for .onion addresses.
SOCKS5 proxy address. Use 127.0.0.1 for Orbot running on this device.
Leave empty to omit from advertisement
Optional: For debugging only. Keep short (max 8 chars recommended) or leave empty to maximize BLE advertisement reliability.
Fastest device discovery, higher battery usage
Default discovery speed and battery usage
Manually configure scan and advertising intervals
Delay between scans while discovering new devices
Delay between scans when no new devices are being found
How long each scan listens for nearby devices
Warning: scan duration ≥ active interval. High duty-cycle scanning will increase battery usage.
How often to restart advertising in case Android silently stopped it
Allows other Reticulum nodes to connect to this device. Useful for Yggdrasil connectivity or when this device should act as a hub.
IP address to bind to. Use 0.0.0.0 to listen on all interfaces.
TCP port to listen on for incoming connections.
This is a demonstration of the Kotlin UI layer communicating with the Reticulum abstraction layer.
Click the buttons above to test the Reticulum abstraction layer.
Since the board type could not be determined automatically, please select it manually in the next step.
If your device was not detected automatically, you can manually select the board type in the next step.
Connect your RNode device via USB
Connect an RNode device via USB and tap refresh

microReticulum runs an embedded Reticulum transport stack on the device itself. It operates as a standalone transport node and will not pair with Columba as a radio interface.
After flashing, you will be prompted to configure the radio parameters for transport mode.
If you have already flashed the firmware externally, you can skip to provisioning the EEPROM.
Click a frequency band to confirm your selection
Select the frequency band that matches your regional regulations
No firmware available. Connect to the internet to download.
Firmware will be downloaded when you proceed
Select a firmware version above to continue
Your RNode has been updated successfully
1. Try disconnecting and reconnecting the device 2. Ensure the USB cable supports data transfer 3. Put the device into bootloader mode manually 4. Try using a different USB port
The flash operation was cancelled before completion
Your device may be in an inconsistent state. Consider reflashing to ensure proper operation.
Sender local time, may differ from yours
Message was successfully delivered to recipient
Single packet delivery for small messages, no link required
Link-based delivery with retries, supports large messages
Delivered via relay node for offline recipients
Only TCP interfaces can be added currently
RNS will discover and auto-connect up to %1$d interfaces from the network.
Discovery is active but auto-connect is disabled. Useful for debugging.
Enable to automatically discover and connect to interfaces announced by other RNS nodes.
These interfaces will auto-detach once discovered interfaces connect.
Interfaces announced by other nodes will appear here once discovery is active.
Yggdrasil is an encrypted IPv6 mesh network. To connect to this interface, you need the Yggdrasil app installed and running on your device. Its available on F-Droid and Google Play.

I2P (Invisible Internet Project) is an anonymous overlay network. To connect to this interface, you need an I2P daemon running on your device. Install the I2P or i2pd app from F-Droid and start it before connecting.
Tap + to add your first network interface
Are you sure you want to delete "%1$s"? This action cannot be undone.
This may take a few seconds
An error occurred while applying configuration changes:
Your changes have been saved to the database. Try applying them again, or restart the app.
Automatically discover peers on local network
Connect to a remote Reticulum transport node
Direct connection to Columba users and Linux ble-reticulum devices
Connects to separate RNode hardware via BLE or Bluetooth Classic
Accept incoming connections from other Reticulum nodes
Discovery enabled - no interfaces found yet
Tap to configure RNS 1.1.x interface discovery
They will be able to send you messages again. Their conversation will reappear if it was not deleted.
Are you sure you want to delete "%1$s"? This action cannot be undone.
Enter a display name for the identity extracted from the Sideband backup.
Create your first identity to start using Columba, or import an existing identity from a backup file.
Enter a display name for this identity. You can use this to manage different personas or use cases.
This will restore the identity from the backup file you selected.
Enter a display name for the imported identity.
Paste a Base32-encoded identity key (e.g., from Sideband).
This key can be imported in Sideband or another Columba instance.
Choose how to import your identity:
Switching to "%1$s" will restart the network service.
You will see only the conversations, contacts, and messages associated with this identity.
Export this identity first if you want to back it up.
Another Reticulum app may be using this interface. Close other apps or disable this interface in Settings.
This interface is currently offline and not passing traffic.
Check that the device is powered on, in range, and properly configured.
Failed to start - tap for details
Tap to view full identity details and QR code for sharing
BLE connections are not available while using a shared Reticulum instance. Only Columba's own instance can initiate Bluetooth LE connections.
Scan this QR code to add me as a contact
Service control is disabled while using a shared Reticulum instance. The network service is managed by another app (e.g., Sideband).
Manually stop or restart the background Reticulum service.
This will stop the background Reticulum service. You will not receive messages until you restart the app or manually restart the service.
You will receive notifications based on your preferences below
Select which events you want to be notified about
Notify when you receive a new message
Notify when you receive a message from a saved peer
Notify when you hear a new announce from a peer
Only notify for direct (1-hop) announces from nearby peers
Skip announces received via TCP interfaces
Notify when a Bluetooth LE peer connects
Notify when a Bluetooth LE peer disconnects
What would you like to do?
This will clear the saved radio configuration and reset the device. It will return to normal host-controlled mode.
Transport mode has been disabled. The device will restart in normal mode.
Failed to disable transport mode. Make sure the device is connected and try again.
Update or install RNode firmware on this device
Set up this device as a Reticulum interface
Set radio parameters for standalone transport mode
Clear saved config and return to normal mode
Point camera at QR code It will scan automatically
This contact is already in your contacts list as "%1$s".
This contact is already in your contacts list.
Turn on Bluetooth to see BLE connections
Enable Bluetooth to discover and connect to nearby BLE peers.
BLE peers will appear here when connected
Choose a codec profile based on your connection speed

Columba uses Bluetooth Low Energy (BLE) to communicate with nearby devices in a mesh network.
To enable this functionality, we need the following permissions:
• Bluetooth Scan: To discover nearby Reticulum peers • Bluetooth Connect: To establish connections with peers • Bluetooth Advertise: To broadcast your presence to other devices
These permissions allow Columba to create a decentralized, off-grid communication network.

To share your location with contacts while the app is in the background, Columba needs the "Allow all the time" permission.
This lets Columba continue sending your encrypted location to chosen contacts even when you switch to another app or lock your screen.
On the next screen, please select "Allow all the time".
Allow location access so Columba can share your live location with contacts and show nearby location updates on the map.
You can add a nickname to easily identify this contact.
Allow access to show recent photos
Scan this QR code to add me as a contact
Show your QR code for others to scan

Bluetooth permissions have been denied. To use BLE features in Columba, please grant Bluetooth permissions in your device settings.
Without these permissions, Columba cannot discover or connect to nearby devices via Bluetooth.
This will reveal your identity to the node operator. The page will refresh after identifying.
Your display name is shown to other peers when you send announces and messages.
Customize your profile icon that others will see. This is compatible with Sideband and other Reticulum apps.
Tap to change your custom icon
Scan to add %1$s as a contact

Your identity hash is a unique cryptographic fingerprint derived from your public key.
Think of it like a username that: - Cannot be changed or duplicated - Proves messages came from you - Allows others to verify your identity

Your LXMF destination hash is derived from your identity and is used for sending and receiving messages.
This is what peers use to: - Send you messages - Find you on the network - Add you as a contact

Your public key is the cryptographic key that enables secure messaging.
It is safe to share publicly and allows others to: - Encrypt messages only you can read - Verify your identity - Establish secure communication

Add me on Reticulum:
Name: %1$s Destination: %2$s
Scan QR code or use: %3$s

Add me on Reticulum:
Name: %1$s
Scan QR code or use: %2$s
Scan this QR code to add me as a contact
Step 1: Scan to connect to hotspot
Have the other person scan this QR code
No WiFi? Share by creating a temporary hotspot instead.
Permission needed to create a WiFi hotspot for sharing.
On the other phone, scan the first QR code to connect to the hotspot
Once connected, scan the second QR code to open the download page
Both phones must be on the same WiFi network
Open the camera app on the other phone and scan the QR code above
Tap the link to open it in a browser
Use Bluetooth, Nearby Share, or any other installed sharing app.
Are you sure you want to exit? Any unsaved progress will be lost.
Your identity is generated and stored securely on your device. You control it completely.
Choose a display name others will see:
You can change this anytime, or create multiple identities for different contexts.
Select the networks you\'d like to use:
You can configure these later in Settings
Share your identity QR code to let others add you as a contact.
This name will be visible to other peers when you send messages and announces.
You can change this later in Settings
Tap the star icon on any peer in the Announce Stream to save them here for quick access.
Grant permission to connect to the USB device
Someone adds you as a contact
Receive messages even when phone is idle
Select a community server or enter custom connection details.
Auto-disconnect once better connections are discovered
Route through SOCKS5 proxy. Required for .onion addresses. Orbot must be installed and connected.
Choose a preset that matches your range and speed needs. These are compatible with Meshtastic naming conventions.
